summary: Excellent soundboard capture of the legendary Feb 28 1973 show, highlighted by a monster second set (The Other One, Truckin', Eyes of the World, Morning Dew) and crystal‑clear audio.
review: The recording is an SBD source that most reviewers describe as crisp, balanced and often “A+” quality, with only minor EQ quirks. Musically the concert is praised for its cohesive energy, especially the soaring The Other One jam, the long Truckin’ > The Other One > Eyes of the World > Morning Dew sequence and the powerful second‑set transitions.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give high marks for both performance (standout jams, tight set flow) and sound quality (A+ SBD). Only a few average or negative comments appear, so the combined weighted score is well above average.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crystal‑clear with great balance; some reviewers note it isn’t the sharpest SBD ever and suggest a slight EQ tweak (lower Phil’s bass), plus an occasional pitch issue on Promised Land. Overall regarded as superior to other available sources.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
poor_songs: Promised Land (slightly flubbed in one source)
show_quality.setlist_flow: Reviewers describe the concert as tightly knit from the first note, with energy building through a tight first set and exploding into an epic second set. Seamless segues (e.g., China>Rider, Truckin'>The Other One>Eyes>Dew) keep momentum high, delivering a cohesive, uplifting experience.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His lead guitar solo on Cold Rain & Snow is called “perfect”; backing vocals on Promised Land and strong lead vocal work throughout receive praise.
band_member_comments.Phil: Intro to The Other One noted as loud/strong; his bass‑drum bombs drive the jam; harmonies on Row Jimmy highlighted as stellar.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s performance on El Paso singled out as “pretty much perfect”; overall keyboard balance praised though one reviewer suggests a slight mix adjustment (lower Phil).
band_member_comments.Drums: Bass & drums section described as short but interesting; drum work is solid and supportive throughout the jams.
